title: VTA and PnO Viral Injections With Optic Fiber Implantation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bogwo","text":"This protocol describes stereotaxic viral injections into VTA and/or PnO followed by implantation of optical fibers in the same surgical session. Implants are fixed using dental cement. No skull screws are used for the fiber implant headcap.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"f4vyx","text":"- All materials listed in the injections-only protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"n4kap","text":"- Optical fibers and ferrules appropriate for the experiment (e.g., 200 μm NA 0.39 or 400 μm NA 0.66).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nwskp","text":"- Dental cement for implant fixation (no skull screws).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ho6an","text":"- Patch cords and mating sleeves for later behavioral and recording sessions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Induce and maintain isoflurane anesthesia (3-4% induction; 1.0-1.5% maintenance). Place the mouse in the stereotaxic frame on a 37 C heating pad.
Expose and level the skull. Drill craniotomies for VTA and/or PnO injections and for the planned implant target(s).
Perform viral injections into VTA and/or PnO at the specified coordinates using slow infusion and a brief post-injection dwell time before retraction.
Implant the fiber(s) to the target depth: either bilateral PnO fibers using a 15 degree lateral approach (DV -4.25 mm) or a VTA fiber above VTA (DV -4.00 mm), depending on the experiment.
Fix the fiber implant(s) using dental cement (no skull screws) and allow cement to fully cure.
Close any remaining exposed scalp margins around the implant base as appropriate.
Monitor until recovery from anesthesia and provide post-operative care.
creator: Cristian González-Cabrera
